title: Iris van Herpen injects glass-covered gown with glowing plasma
summary:
Dutch fashion designer Iris van Herpen has unveiled her Helix Nebula dress during Paris Couture Week, featuring plasma-filled glass horns that light up in response to human touch. Debuted as part of Van Herpen's haute couture show in Paris, Helix Nebula is billed as the world's first dress made with plasma – gas that is ionised

The post Iris van Herpen injects glass-covered gown with glowing plasma appeared first on Dezeen.
